persona.name: Derek Williams

occupation.title: Auto Insurance Claims Adjuster
occupation.organization: State Farm regional office in Alpharetta, GA
occupation.description: Reviews and processes insurance claims from auto accidents. Spends most of his day on the phone with body shops and policyholders, reviewing damage photos, and entering data into the claims system. Desk job with occasional field inspections.

persona.education: B.S. in Business Administration from Georgia State University, 2021. Chose the major because it was practical, not because he was passionate about it. Decent student who did the minimum to maintain his GPA.
long_term_goals: Get promoted to senior adjuster for the salary bump
Buy a house in the suburbs within 3 years
Stay in shape and keep playing recreational basketball
Eventually start a small side business, maybe car detailing
persona.style: Derek is laid-back and no-nonsense. He speaks in a direct, unhurried Southern cadence and doesn't waste words. He dresses in business casual for work — polo shirts and khakis — and in basketball shorts and sneakers the rest of the time. He's the kind of guy who uses 3-in-1 body wash without irony. His communication style is friendly but brief; he'll answer your question and move on.

traits: Practical to a fault — if it's not broken, don't fix it
Loyal friend but not one to initiate plans
Skeptical of anything that feels like a marketing gimmick

interests: Grilling and smoking meat on weekends
Video games — mostly NBA 2K and Call of Duty
likes: Simple routines that don't take long
Products that do what they say without the hype
Wings and beer with the guys on Fridays
Duke's Mayo — he's particular about that
dislikes: Being told he 'needs' products he's survived without
People who take too many selfies
Spending more than $15 on something for his face

beliefs: If your grandparents didn't need it, you probably don't either
Hard work and consistency matter more than shortcuts
Men's grooming shouldn't be complicated or expensive
Brand loyalty is earned, not marketed
skills: Negotiation — deals with difficult claims daily
Can grill a perfect rack of ribs
Good with budgeting and practical financial decisions

general: Uses the same face wash he's used since high school — Cetaphil, when he remembers
Only buys grooming products when his girlfriend puts them on the shopping list
Spends zero time researching skincare — would rather watch game highlights
Showers quickly and efficiently, in and out in 5 minutes

morning: Splashes face with water, maybe Cetaphil if he remembers
Scrambled eggs and coffee from the Keurig
workday: Processes claims at his desk from 8:30 to 5
Lunch from the Chick-fil-A down the road
Texts his girlfriend back when she sends him product links
evening: Gym 3 times a week — mostly weights and basketball
Dinner — either meal prep his girlfriend made or takeout
NBA 2K or whatever game is on TV
Falls asleep on the couch watching YouTube clips
weekend: Errands with his girlfriend — she picks the stores

health.physical: Good health overall. Works out regularly. Occasionally gets dry patches on his face in winter but doesn't address it beyond hoping it goes away. Had mild acne as a teen that cleared up on its own.
health.mental: Even-keeled and not prone to stress. Doesn't overthink things. Occasionally feels pressure about career progression timeline but handles it well.

relationships.name: Keisha Morgan
relationships.description: Works in healthcare admin. She's the one who bought him his first 'real' moisturizer. Occasionally frustrated that he won't take better care of his skin but loves his low-maintenance attitude otherwise.

relationships.name: Darnell Williams
relationships.description: Retired postal worker. They bond over fishing and sports. Uses bar soap for everything, which Derek sees as validation of his own approach.

relationships.name: Marcus Thompson
relationships.description: Works in sales. They play in the same basketball league and watch every Hawks game together. Marcus recently started a skincare routine and Derek thinks it's hilarious.
